target.title: Add hosted catalog source profile validation
target.body:
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:start -->## Full hosted marketplace feed stackWe are pausing this stack at PR8 for Vincent's review and takeover/shaping before wiring hosted marketplace data into startup, install, or automatic refresh behavior.- PR1 [#95846](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95846): refactors the bundled external plugin catalog toward the feed-shaped data model.- PR2 [#95868](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95868): adds the lazy hosted marketplace feed loader for the configured public feed URL, with guarded HTTPS fetch, checksum, schema, size, timeout, and bundled fallback behavior.- PR3 [#95877](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95877): adds accepted hosted snapshot fallback so a failed or unchanged hosted fetch can reuse the last valid feed payload.- PR4 [#95964](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95964): persists hosted marketplace feed snapshots in OpenClaw state.- PR5 [#95969](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95969): adds marketplace source profile validation for hosted feed install candidates.- PR6 [#95981](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95981): adds configurable `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources` profiles.- PR7 [#96155](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96155): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` as the explicit manual snapshot refresh path.- PR8 [#96158](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96158): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` as the explicit manual read path over hosted, snapshot, or bundled fallback entries.Next after review, not in this stack yet: decide whether PR9 should wire marketplace entries into install/search/startup, add automatic conditional refresh using `ETag` / `Last-Modified`, or move to ClawHub producer-side validation for `/v1/feeds/plugins` and `/v1/feeds/skills`.<!-- hosted-feed-stack:end -->## StackStep 5 in the hosted feed rollout. This is stacked on `feeds-hosted-catalog-persistent-snapshot` after:- #95846 reshapes the bundled external plugin catalog as a feed-shaped fallback- #95868 adds the guarded hosted feed loader- #95877 adds the last-known-good snapshot fallback contract- #95964 persists hosted catalog snapshots in OpenClaw state## RFC alignmentThis PR adds the first local feed/source profile scaffolding from RFC #19. The important boundary is that profile names are local OpenClaw configuration. A hosted feed can refer to a configured `sourceRef`, but it cannot define registry domains, credentials, or trust roots for the client.This now matches the live ClawHub plugin feed at `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`, which uses `public-clawhub` candidates. The PR also keeps the older register host allowed for transition, while `registry.openclaw.ai` is still being brought online.## Summary- Adds local profile types for hosted catalog feeds and package sources- Adds the default `clawhub-public` feed profile pointing at `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`- Adds the default `public-clawhub` source profile for ClawHub candidates and keeps `public-npm` for npm-backed candidates- Lets named local feed profiles authorize their configured HTTPS host through the existing SSRF guard path- Keeps direct ad hoc `feedUrl` overrides constrained to the public host allowlist, including `clawhub.ai` and the earlier register host- Filters feed entries that opt into `install.candidates[].sourceRef` when the source ref is missing or not configured locally- Applies the same source-ref filtering to hosted responses and hosted snapshot fallback- Recognizes RFC-style `id` and `title` fields for catalog identity and labels so live ClawHub entries are not dropped before the later install-mapping slice## Deliberately not included- Feed/source profile persistence in user config files- Secret-reference resolution or authenticated feed/source access- Signed feed envelopes or trust-key configuration- Scheduled refresh, `maxStale`, or stale-catalog status reporting- Changing plugin install execution to consume candidate source profiles or translating RFC candidates into installer specs- Search, install UX, skills, or telemetry## Validation- `pnpm exec oxfmt src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `git diff --check`- `CI=1 timeout 180s node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
This PR validates hosted marketplace install source refs against local source profiles and accepts the live ClawHub plugin feed format now served as `schemaVersion: 2`.

Real environment tested:
WSL Ubuntu 24.04, `/root/src/openclaw-feeds-hosted-catalog-prep`, PR5 and cumulative hosted marketplace feed stack through PR8 at `13cea8e00e7`, built source tree with `pnpm build`, isolated OpenClaw state via `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof.rKXnUX`, and the live ClawHub feed endpoint `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. On PR5: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts --reporter=dot`
2. On PR8 after rebasing PR6-PR8: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-entries.test.ts src/plugins/marketplace.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.marketplaces.test.ts --reporter=dot`
3. `curl -sS -D - https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins -o /tmp/clawhub-plugins.json`
4. `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-feeds-proof.rKXnUX node openclaw.mjs plugins marketplace entries --feed-url https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`

Evidence after fix:
PR5 focused catalog tests passed: `1 passed / 38 tests`. Full stack focused tests passed across 3 Vitest shards: `1 passed / 8 tests`, `2 passed / 7 tests`, and `2 passed / 70 tests`. Live curl showed `HTTP/1.1 200 OK`, `X-Content-Sha256: 99b2608a539fe54d0d0ded2182574b62b8b772bd2011d9eb497147be23ff2c2e`, `Last-Modified: Wed, 24 Jun 2026 13:06:20 GMT`, and a body beginning with `{ "schemaVersion": 2, "id": "clawhub-official" ... }`. The built CLI returned `Source: hosted`, `Entries: 59`, `Feed: clawhub-official sequence 9`, and live ClawHub plugin entries.

Observed result after fix:
The live ClawHub v2 feed is accepted, entries with `public-clawhub` source refs resolve to `clawhub:` and npm install choices, and unsupported future schema versions still stay out of legacy parsing in tests.

What was not tested:
No live install was run. This PR validates and lists feed metadata only; install wiring remains a later slice.
target.author: giodl73-repo
